title: Officials: Man who shot 2 in Hampton Beach before dying by suicide was active-duty Navy sailor
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A US Navy sailor shot two people in Hampton, NH, before dying by suicide, officials said.
The shooter, identified as 28-year-old Tyshawn Cooper, was assigned to the USS Hampton submarine.
The incident is being investigated by New Hampshire State Police and the Naval Criminal Investigative Service.

title: Report: Celtics’ Paul George turned down $3.9 million trade bonus
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Paul George waived his $3.9 million trade bonus, keeping his salary cap hit at $54.1 million.
George's decision provides the Boston Celtics cap relief as they navigate the salary cap and luxury tax.
The Celtics aim to avoid severe penalties by staying out of the second luxury tax apron.

title: R.I. food bank thanks Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce for pre-wedding donation
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce donated $1 million to Rhode Island Community Food Bank before their wedding.
The donation will aid the food bank in supporting 137 member agencies and combat growing food assistance needs.
Swift and Kelce also donated to various charities in New York and Pennsylvania, including Feeding America.

title: 5 takeaways from the Celtics’ press conference about the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to Philadelphia for Paul George and draft assets, citing financial and strategic reasons.
Celtics pursued Giannis Antetokounmpo before trading Brown, but the offer was declined, leading to the deal with George.
Despite Brown's 10-year tenure and success, he felt disrespected by trade talks; Celtics management apologized for his feelings.

title: 5 takeaways from the U.S. loss to Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The U.S. lost 4-1 to Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16, ending their tournament run in Seattle.
Belgium's Charles De Ketelaere scored twice; costly errors by U.S. players led to two more Belgian goals.
Key changes in Belgium's lineup included replacing stars De Bruyne and Doku; the U.S. lineup remained consistent.

title: 2 siblings drown in Vermont river, brother tried to save younger sister, police say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sandro Lala, 25, and his sister Yazmin Yupangui, 11, drowned in Vermont’s Lamoille River while fishing last week.
Yupangui fell into the river; Lala attempted to save her, but both disappeared and were reported missing.
A GoFundMe raised $70,000 for funeral expenses and transport to Ecuador, reflecting community support for their family.

title: City declines to use reserve funds to prevent BPS layoffs as more than 560 positions are cut
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu declined to use reserve funds, leading to over 560 Boston Public Schools positions being cut.
The layoffs are due to a $1.7 billion budget approved by city council amid a 3,000-student enrollment decline in Boston schools.
BPS aims to place affected educators in new positions, maintaining a 10:1 student-to-teacher ratio and adjusting staffing levels.

title: Harmful bacteria persist in Merrimack River days after Haverhill sewer break
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Elevated bacteria levels persist in the Merrimack River after a Haverhill sewage spill despite an emergency bypass installation.
Four of the seven testing sites, including Plum Island Light, show fecal coliform and E. coli above safe recreational levels.
Shellfish harvesting areas in several towns remain closed, and DMF is increasing testing to ensure public health standards.

title: Boater remains missing after crash off of Salisbury Beach
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: One man is still missing after a boat crash into a jetty off Salisbury Beach on Friday night.
Two people were rescued and hospitalized following the Friday night crash near Salisbury Beach State Reservation.
Coast Guard suspended its search for the missing North Reading man, identified as 49-year-old Vilmar Dorner.

title: After officiating Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding, Adam Sandler played pickup basketball in this Mass. vacation hotspot
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Adam Sandler officiated Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding at Madison Square Garden on Friday.
Sandler played pickup basketball in Nantucket and took photos with locals after the wedding weekend.
Sandler often joins random basketball games and previously played with the BU men's team and Kevin Garnett.
